target: data/sections.json part2.html sign-only-non-discrimination: (1) fix Counter(2) example list attribution one-to-one removing WIN-060 and routing it through Counter(3); (2) tighten anticipated-counter-pivot reply to remove convention-dependent hemisphere partition phrasing

patches.description: ISS-2011: Restructure Counter (2) example list to one-to-one WIN attribution. Remove 'a westward shift coordinate' (WIN-060 does not cite a specific magnitude); keep drift rate (WIN-059) and +0.34 W/m²/decade (WIN-066). Add explicit routing note that WIN-060 goes through Counter (3).
patches.find_pattern: (2) Cited value commits the dome to magnitude: when the dome's WIN cites a specific number (a drift rate, a westward shift coordinate, a +0.34 W/m²/decade), the WIN is staked on that number — disclaiming the magnitude post-hoc is a retreat from the WIN's own framing, not a clarification of it.
patches.replace_pattern: (2) Cited value commits the dome to magnitude: when the dome's WIN cites a specific number — a drift rate (WIN-059) or a +0.34 W/m²/decade trend (WIN-066) — the WIN is staked on that number; disclaiming the magnitude post-hoc is a retreat from the WIN's own framing, not a clarification of it. WIN-060 cites only a direction, not a specific magnitude, and routes through Counter (3) instead.

patches.description: ISS-2012: Tighten anticipated-counter-pivot reply to remove 'continental land-mass percentages' (convention-dependent ~39%/~19% hemisphere split). Replace with 'continental land-mass and ocean distributions' plus explicit 'empirical quantities measurable independent of which projection you display them in' framing per ISS-2012 suggested_fix.
patches.find_pattern: continental land-mass percentages, observatory readings of the geomagnetic field, CERES top-of-atmosphere energy fluxes — are direct measurements independent of the geometry dispute. The standard model derives the magnitudes from those observables; the dome adopts the numbers. The mechanism is not
patches.replace_pattern: continental land-mass and ocean distributions, observatory magnetic-field readings, and CERES top-of-atmosphere fluxes — empirical quantities measurable independent of which projection you display them in. The standard model derives the magnitudes from those observables; the dome adopts the numbers. The mechanism is not

tldr_evidence: ISS-2011: WIN-060 (SAA westward cell shift) does not cite a specific drift-rate magnitude; the dome's claim is directional only. Counter (2) as written misleads by including it alongside the drift rate (WIN-059) and W/m²/decade trend (WIN-066), which do cite numbers. ISS-2012: 'continental land-mass percentages' implicitly imports the ~39% NH vs ~19% SH partition, which depends on hemisphere-convention definition — vulnerable to a dome defender pivot. Observatory readings and CERES fluxes are directly measurable and independent of projection choice.
tldr_verdict: Both patches are narrow prose precision improvements. No argument structure changes; no verdict changes. ISS-2011 prevents a legitimate defender nitpick that Counter (2) misrepresents WIN-060. ISS-2012 forecloses a convention-dependence escape hatch in the counter-pivot reply.
